div.h2: poll/epoll Stack: From .poll to the nginx Event Loop
div.p: Your 4-line mydevice_poll() is mechanically identical to TCP socket poll — same kernel code path, different file_operations table.

Key Insight poll_wait(filp, &mydevice_read_queue, wait) is the exact mechanism used by TCP sockets, pipes, timerfd, and every other pollable fd in Linux. The poll_table *wait argument carries the subscription context — whether called from select(2), poll(2), or epoll_ctl(EPOLL_CTL_ADD), poll_wait() does the right thing. Your 4-line .poll function is the foundation beneath nginx's 50k connections.

text.tspan: → poll_wait() stores epitem in wait queue
→ NOT called again on every epoll_wait()
returns: array of {fd, events} for ready fds
only fds that fired wake_up appear here
resumed by libuv when epoll_wait returns
no locks needed at application level
resumes when epoll fires the fd
